original_statement: "Across its 10,000 tournament simulations, England made it through to the knockout stage 96.0% of the time [from Group L containing Croatia, Ghana and Panama in the 2026 FIFA World Cup] and won Group L in 67.9% of the sims."
statement_topic: FIFA World Cup 2026 - Group stage qualification
statement_context: Excerpt from comprehensive tournament simulation analysis published by The Analyst on 2026-06-01. Author Andrew Beasley using Opta supercomputer methodology with 10,000 simulations.
resolution_criteria: England finishes in the top two of Group L in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, as determined by the official FIFA group stage standings after the final group match on or before 2026-06-26.
source_text_snippet: Across its 10,000 tournament simulations, England made it through to the knockout stage 96.0% of the time and won Group L in 67.9% of the sims. They are the third-likeliest side to win their group at the 2026 World Cup behind only Spain (75.3%) and Argentina (73.0%).
